name: Possible Local Worm Detected
notes: This rule triggers when a local host is generating reconnaissance or suspicious events across a large number of hosts (greater than 300) in 20 minutes. This could indicate the presence of a worm on the network or a wide spread scan.
bb_references: BB:CategoryDefinition: Worm Events with the same source IP more than 5 times
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:HostDefinition: Servers
when the event context is Local to Local, Local to Remote

when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Recon Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Suspicious Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Worm Events with the same source IP more than 5 times, across more than 300 destination IP within 20 minutes
thresholds: when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Recon Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Suspicious Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Worm Events with the same source IP more than 5 times, across more than 300 destination IP within 20 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Possible Local Worm Detected on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND NOT when an event matches any of the following BB:HostDefinition: Servers
AND when the event context is Local to Local, Local to Remote

AND when any of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Recon Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Suspicious Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Worm Events with the same source IP more than 5 times, across more than 300 destination IP within 20 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Possible Local Worm Detected

Event description: Reports a local host generating reconnaissance or suspicious events across a large number of hosts (greater than 300) in 20 minutes. This may indicate the presence of a worm on the network or a wide spread scan.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
